summary: The OAuth2 implementation is architecturally sound with good separation of concerns, but two high-severity bugs need fixing before merge: the concurrent token refresh race condition and the silent decryption failure. The email case-sensitivity issue should also be addressed to prevent user-facing duplicate account problems.

issues.description: Concurrent refreshAccessToken calls for the same user can invalidate the stored refresh token, leaving the user in a broken auth state that persists until manual re-authentication.
issues.resolution: Implement a per-user mutex using a database advisory lock: SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(userId || provider)) at the start of the refresh transaction. This serializes concurrent refreshes for the same user without affecting different users.

issues.description: decryptToken silently returns empty string on decryption failure instead of throwing, causing downstream code to send invalid tokens to OAuth providers.
issues.resolution: Remove the catch-and-return-empty pattern. Let decryption errors propagate as DecryptionError. Add a specific catch in the caller (refreshAccessToken) that triggers a full re-authentication flow when decryption fails.

issues.description: Profile linking fails when OAuth providers return different email casing for the same user, creating duplicate accounts.
issues.resolution: Normalize email with .toLowerCase().trim() before all database operations. Add a migration to normalize existing emails and create a case-insensitive unique index.

issues.description: State parameter has a 5-minute replay window. While the authorization code is single-use at the provider level, the state validation alone doesn't prevent replay.
issues.resolution: Switch from time-based expiry to immediate deletion after use. Store states in a Set or Redis with delete-on-read semantics.

issues.description: Magic number 10000 (timeout in ms) in exchangeCodeForTokens should be a named constant.
issues.resolution: Extract to const OAUTH_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000 at module level.

recommendations: Add a concurrency testing pattern to the project's test utilities — the token refresh race condition could have been caught by the dev agent with a simple Promise.all test helper
Document the token encryption contract in CLAUDE.md (must throw on failure, never return empty) so future agents don't reintroduce the silent failure pattern
Consider adding a database-locking utility to the project since concurrent access patterns will recur in other features — avoid reimplementing advisory locks per feature
The BA's acceptance criteria were excellent — every one was directly testable. The domain glossary helped all downstream agents use consistent terminology
